title: How do the biggest black holes in the universe form? Ripples in spacetime provide a clue
summary: Ripples in spacetime suggest that merging black holes and neutron stars have unusual oval orbits before colliding, challenging existing physics laws. This discovery provides clues about the formation of the universe's largest black holes.

title: Artemis 2 moon launch brought nearly 350,000 people to Florida's Space Coast
summary: The Artemis 2 moon launch attracted nearly 350,000 visitors to Florida's Space Coast. This turnout was roughly equivalent to the population of Honolulu, Hawaii.

title: Should Saturn's huge moon Titan be humanity's next destination, after the moon and Mars?
summary: A first-of-its-kind gathering next month will plan a future crewed mission to Titan, Saturn's largest moon. The event aims to explore Titan as a potential destination after the Moon and Mars.

title: How an exoplanet odd couple survived by traveling in from the cold together
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope discovered that a mini-Neptune exoplanet formed far from its star and migrated inward, providing insight into the origins of similar exoplanets. This finding could explain the prevalence of mini-Neptunes in other star systems.

title: Europe's 1st reusable spacecraft 'Space Rider' clears key hurdles on the road to launch
summary: Europe's first reusable spacecraft, ESA's 'Space Rider,' has cleared significant test milestones, moving closer to its inaugural flight. The vehicle is undergoing rigorous qualifications to achieve flight readiness.

title: 'Whatever Russia is testing, it's sophisticated': 2 Russian satellites get within 10 feet of each other in orbit
summary: Two Russian satellites recently demonstrated advanced orbital maneuvering skills by approaching within 10 feet of each other. This proximity operation suggests sophisticated testing capabilities.

title: 'Thor' hammered down 15 years ago, and it's still the only Marvel movie to do the God of Thunder justice
summary: The article humorously compares the 2011 Marvel movie 'Thor' to the God of Thunder's legacy, noting it as the only film to do the character justice. It reflects on the movie's impact and subsequent reception.

title: Sci-fi RPG 'Exodus''s combat looks and feels like 'Mass Effect', but the dialogue system could be a big upgrade (video)
summary: The article discusses the combat mechanics of the sci-fi RPG 'Exodus', comparing it to 'Mass Effect', while highlighting potential improvements in its dialogue system. The update was shared by developers Jun Aslan and Phaedra from Archetype Entertainment.

title: Nikon Action 7x50 binoculars review
summary: Nikon has released a new set of Action 7x50 binoculars aimed at astronomy novices. The review highlights their suitability for beginners in the field.

title: Cornell Students Aid NASA with Drone Safety in Sky
summary: Cornell University students are developing a national air transportation management system for safe drone operations, supported by a NASA grant through the University Student Research Challenge.

title: A Sea of Spinning Clouds
summary: Peter I Island in West Antarctica caused atmospheric disturbances visible as spinning clouds. The event was observed from space, showcasing unique atmospheric phenomena.

title: Ames Science Stars of the Month May 2026
summary: NASA Ames Science Directorate recognized four individuals—Lora Jovanović, Tammy Moore, Frances Donovan, and Jaden Ta—for their outstanding contributions in May 2026. Their work highlights the entrepreneurial spirit and technical expertise driving NASA's missions.

title: NASA’s Dryden Aeronautical Test Range Supports Flight, Space Missions
summary: NASA's Dryden Aeronautical Test Range (DATR) at Armstrong Flight Research Center supports flight and space missions by providing essential communications, tracking, and data services. The range plays a critical role in enabling safe and effective experimental aircraft and flight research.

title: NASA Wallops to Host Public Information Session May 13
summary: NASA Wallops Flight Facility will host a public information session on May 13 to discuss activities such as causeway bridge construction, beach replenishment, and updates from the GLOBE program. Federal and state health experts will also participate in the event.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for SpaceX 34th Station Resupply Launch, Arrival
summary: NASA and SpaceX are targeting May 12 for the 34th SpaceX resupply mission to the International Space Station, carrying 6,500 pounds of cargo. This marks another commercial resupply service mission under NASA's contract with SpaceX.

title: Unlocking the Mystery of X-ray Dots
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory discovered a new 'X-ray dot' that may help explain the nature of hundreds or thousands of similar objects. The finding was announced alongside an artist's illustration released on April 28, 2026.

title: NASA’s Roman Poised to Transform Hunt for Elusive Neutron Stars
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope may help detect elusive neutron stars, which are typically invisible despite being scattered throughout the Milky Way. A new study suggests the telescope's capabilities could reveal these stellar remnants.

title: New NASA Technology Mimics Extreme Cold of the Lunar Night
summary: NASA is developing new technology to withstand the extreme cold of lunar nights, which can cause materials like rubber to shatter and electronics to fail. This research aims to support future missions to the Moon, Mars, and beyond.

title: Melting Snow Off Shivelyuch
summary: Near-constant volcanic activity is observed at Shiveluch in Russia, causing melting snow. The situation is being monitored closely.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion as Flight 12 approaches, six months after the completion of Flight 11. The old design of Pad 1 is being upgraded.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission, potentially marking a calm period before an upcoming surge in spaceflight activity.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its 12th Starship flight test with a revised trajectory. The company is preparing for the return of Starship launches within weeks.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully launched its new Soyuz-5 rocket on its maiden flight from the Baikonur Cosmodrome. The debut marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A NASA astronaut and doctor discusses their upcoming mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The astronaut has experience in mass casualty events and military service overseas.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. The mission marks the return of the heavy-lift vehicle, which has not flown since June 2019.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the recent successful Artemis II launch, which sent four astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: SpaceX's Falcon Heavy, ULA's Atlas V, and Arianespace's Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's LEO satellites this week. The launches will occur from Florida, California, and French Guiana.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic clues on Mars, suggesting potential signs of ancient life. The findings mark a significant advancement in the search for extraterrestrial life.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned in the space industry news, but no specific details or context are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical testing phase for spacecraft parachutes, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out,' which involves rigorous thermal and stress testing to ensure reliability during re-entry. This process is essential for validating parachute systems ahead of upcoming missions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could significantly enhance satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully passed space-like tests, confirming it is on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA is inviting SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026. The event will include an online preparatory phase in April-May and an in-person forum on June 8-9, 2026, at ESA Headquarters in Paris.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The location for this achievement has been highlighted as optimal.

title: South Korea Pushes to Commercialize Quantum Research
summary: South Korea is accelerating efforts to commercialize quantum research, aiming to establish itself as a key player in the emerging quantum technology sector. The country is positioning itself to capitalize on the growing global interest in quantum advancements.

title: Overview Energy Wins Air Force Contract
summary: Overview Energy has secured an Air Force contract to provide continuous power to military bases using space-based solar energy. The company aims to mitigate fuel line vulnerabilities by leveraging solar power day and night.

title: Astranis Raises $450M+, Including $300M Series E
summary: Astranis raised over $450 million in a funding round led by Snowpoint Ventures and Franklin Templeton, valuing the company at $2.8 billion. The investment includes a $300 million Series E round.

title: Scout Space Closes $18M Series A
summary: Scout Space has secured $18 million in Series A funding. The company plans to expand by building a new satellite sensor factory near Washington, D.C., and nearly doubling its workforce within 18 months.

title: LiveEO Raises €28M+ to Fund its First Constellation
summary: LiveEO, a German company, has raised over €28 million to fund its first satellite constellation and scale its business to meet increasing demand for Earth observation (EO) data.

title: Commerce Aiming to Open Novel Space Applications This Summer
summary: The Office of Space Commerce (OSC) plans to streamline licensing for innovative space missions by this summer. This initiative aims to simplify the regulatory process for novel space applications.

title: Bridenstine Takes Over as Quantum Space CEO
summary: Jim Bridenstine, former NASA Administrator, has become the CEO of Quantum Space. He expressed his enthusiasm for returning to national security and defense, aligning with his background and interests.

title: Anduril Names Its SBI Team For Golden Dome
summary: Anduril has named its Space-Based Interceptor (SBI) team as part of the Golden Dome missile defense architecture. The company is one of 12 prime contractors contributing to this initiative.

title: ESA Taps Edge Aerospace for Space Cloud Contract
summary: ESA has selected Edge Aerospace, a Luxembourg-based company, to develop an architecture and roadmap for orbital data centers under a new space cloud contract. The agreement aims to advance the concept of data processing in space.

title: Lt. Gen. Schiess Nominated to Lead Space Force
summary: Lt. Gen. Schiess has been nominated to lead the U.S. Space Force. If confirmed, he will become the third chief of space operations since the Space Force's establishment in 2019.

title: Enabled by AI, NGA Director Focused on Mission, Transformation, and Workforce
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) is leveraging AI to enhance its mission, transformation, and workforce to meet evolving threats and warfighter needs. The agency's new director emphasizes resilience and the adoption of emerging technologies.

title: Telespazio to Distribute Greenerwave Multi-Orbit Terminals to European Defense Market
summary: Telespazio France will distribute multi-orbit terminals developed by Greenerwave to the European defense market under a new agreement. The deal aims to enhance connectivity solutions for defense applications.

title: Verizon Details ‘Satellite-Everywhere’ for Disaster Response, Expands Satellite Fleet
summary: Verizon is expanding its satellite fleet to enhance disaster response capabilities and aims for a 'satellite-everywhere' failover strategy. The telco is focusing on improving connectivity during emergencies.

title: Astranis Raises $300M in Series E and Additional Access to Capital
summary: Astranis, a satellite manufacturer, has raised $300 million in a Series E funding round and secured up to $155 million in a credit facility to boost satellite production.

title: SWISSto12 Strikes German Partnership Deal for D2D GEO Satellite
summary: SWISSto12 has formed a new partnership in Germany to enhance media broadcast capabilities for Geostationary (GEO) satellites. The collaboration will focus on developing Direct-to-Device (D2D) satellite technology.

title: Anduril to Work With Impulse Space, K2 Space, Voyager, and Others on Space-Based Interceptor Award
summary: Anduril Industries has formed a team of collaborators including Impulse Space, Inversion Space, K2 Space, and Sandia National Laboratories to develop space-based interceptor technologies. The collaboration follows Anduril's recent award to prototype these technologies.

title: Telesat’s GEO Business Declines 25% in Q1
summary: Telesat's GEO business experienced a 25% decline year-over-year in Q1 2026. The company is focusing on launching its Lightspeed constellation.

title: What Amazon’s Globalstar Acquisition Means for MSS Spectrum and D2D Market: Analyst Roundtable
summary: Amazon acquired Globalstar in a $10.8 billion deal, signaling its expansion into the Mobile Satellite Services (MSS) spectrum and direct-to-device (D2D) market.

title: NRO Makes New Commercial Awards to EarthDaily, Iceye, and Pixxel
summary: The National Reconnaissance Office (NRO) awarded new contracts for Earth sensing data to EarthDaily, Iceye, and Pixxel under its Strategic Commercial Enhancements (SCE) effort. This move expands the NRO's commercial satellite data sources.

title: STMicroelectronics Expects its Space Business to Reach More than $3B Over Three Years
summary: STMicroelectronics, a global semiconductor company, expects its space business to exceed $3 billion in revenue over the next three years. The company supplies components to SpaceX and other space industry players.
